body: public static native int openpty(int[] amaster, int[] aslave, byte[] name, Termios termios, WinSize winsize);
translation: # Python does not have a direct equivalent to the openpty function in C.
# However, you can use the pty module in Python to achieve similar functionality.
# Here is an example of how you might use it:
# The openpty function in C returns two file descriptors, one for the master and one for the slave.
# In Python, the pty.openpty() function returns a pair of file descriptors.
# You can use these file descriptors in the same way as you would use the file descriptors returned by the openpty function in C.
# Note: The pty module is not available on all platforms.
# If you need to use openpty on a platform that does not support the pty module, you will need to use a different approach.
